mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0% over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising policy rates, expansions in industrial production confirm resilient corporate output and earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a negative or zero average forward return across all tightening episodes since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining housing starts signal a sharp contraction in construction demand and financial leverage stress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of cases following three consecutive months of housing starts declines over a 10-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate indicate eroding real yields and impending aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS levels shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (interaction coefficient p > 0.05) across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% during economic recovery phases confirms sustained aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to generate positive 60-day returns in recovery periods, with average forward returns below zero across all post-recession quarters since 2005.
